target.name: Multiverse Computing
target.founder: Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Román Orús, Alfonso Rubio-Manzanares, Sam Mugel

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $215M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Multiverse Computing is a Series B AI & Software company that has raised $215M+ to date and carries a current valuation exceeding $500M. The peer universe comprises five same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom), supplemented by one public comp (Palantir Technologies). This is a narrow but instructive set given the frontier-tech focus.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-Progression, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $215M total raised proxy, implies $408M. Peer-Median anchors to the five-company cohort, which exhibits a median valuation of $5.0B but spans $500M to $32.0B—reflecting extreme dispersion within early-stage AI. This wide range reflects heterogeneity in perceived AI risk, IP strength, and market timing rather than fundamental comparability.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $347M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.7B. The current >$500M valuation sits within this band but near the bottom quartile, suggesting either conservatism or a gap between private and public appetite for frontier AI risk. Key uncertainties include which peer trajectory is most analogous (given the 64x spread in the comp set) and whether stage-progression captures the venture premium in frontier AI. Resolution hinges on revenue traction, model differentiation, and capital efficiency relative to peers.
